generated_oml:
// Sensing Components
instance HokuyoUST10LXSensingComponent : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST10LX 2D LiDAR sensor: 0.04‑3.5 m range, 10 Hz, 0.5° angular resolution."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HokuyoUST10LXDataTransmitted
]

instance IntelD435iDepthSensingComponent : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel D435i depth camera: baseline 0.14 m, depth drift 1 cm over 3 m, 30 Hz."
    DTDFVocab:producedData IntelD435iDepthDataTransmitted
]

instance WheelSpeedSensingComponent : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Wheel speed sensor: 50 Hz, LIN network, measures wheel velocity."
    DTDFVocab:producedData WheelSpeedDataTransmitted
]

instance SteeringAngleSensingComponent : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering angle sensor: 50 Hz, LIN network, measures steering angle."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringAngleDataTransmitted
]

instance BatteryVoltageSensingComponent : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Battery voltage sensor: 50 Hz, LIN network, measures battery voltage."
    DTDFVocab:producedData BatteryVoltageDataTransmitted
]

instance OperatorCommandUIComponent : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "User interface component providing operator commands via WebSocket API."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OperatorCommandDataTransmitted
]

// Data Transmitted
instance HokuyoUST10LXDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Serialized LiDAR point cloud data transmitted from Hokuyo UST10LX."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXSensingComponent
]

instance IntelD435iDepthDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Serialized depth image data transmitted from Intel D435i."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelD435iDepthSensingComponent
]

instance WheelSpeedDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Serialized wheel speed measurement transmitted."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom WheelSpeedSensingComponent
]

instance SteeringAngleDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Serialized steering angle measurement transmitted."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringAngleSensingComponent
]

instance BatteryVoltageDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Serialized battery voltage measurement transmitted."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BatteryVoltageSensingComponent
]

instance OperatorCommandDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Serialized operator command (e.g., enable/disable ACC, target speed)."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OperatorCommandUIComponent
]

// Data
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "LiDAR point cloud data: raw 2D points collected by Hokuyo UST10LX."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreprocessingEnabler, DataTranslationEnabler, ClusteringTrackingEnabler, DataMonitoringEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData HokuyoUST10LXDataTransmitted
]

instance DepthCameraData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Depth camera data: depth slices from Intel D435i."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreprocessingEnabler, DataTranslationEnabler, DataMonitoringEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData IntelD435iDepthDataTransmitted
]

instance WheelSpeedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle wheel speed measurement."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadingEnabler, DataMonitoringEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData WheelSpeedDataTransmitted
]

instance SteeringAngleData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle steering angle measurement."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadingEnabler, DataMonitoringEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringAngleDataTransmitted
]

instance BatteryVoltageData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle battery voltage measurement."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData BatteryVoltageDataTransmitted
]

instance OperatorCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Operator command data received via WebSocket API."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RemoteManagementEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperatorCommandDataTransmitted
]

// Enablers
instance DataPreprocessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data Preprocessing FE: performs DBSCAN clustering on LiDAR and Kalman filtering on depth slices."
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ISO 123247 Data Translation FE: normalises sensor data to common units and performs type alignment."
]

instance ClusteringTrackingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking FE: produces object tracks from processed point clouds."
]

instance DataCollectionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data Collection FE: aggregates telemetry and transmits to PostgreSQL database via RESTful JSON endpoints."
]

instance DataMonitoringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data Monitoring FE: streams, archives, and visualises real‑time vehicle state and object tracks."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ComputationOffloadingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Computation Offloading FE: hosts ACC algorithm on remote server to compute desired speed."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance RemoteManagementEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Remote Management FE: receives operator commands to control ACC."
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

// Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data Monitoring service: streams, archives, and visualises real‑time vehicle state information, projected driving path, and detected object tracks."
    DTDFVocab:provides InsightVehicleState, InsightObjectTracks
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Computation Offloading service: executes ACC algorithm on remote node to produce desired speed."
    DTDFVocab:provides ActionACCTargetSpeed
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Remote Management service: allows operators to enable/disable ACC, set target speed, and issue emergency braking."
    DTDFVocab:provides ActionEnableACC, ActionDisableACC, ActionSetTargetSpeed, ActionEmergencyBrake
]

// Insights
instance InsightVehicleState : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight: current vehicle state (speed, steering angle, battery voltage)."
]

instance InsightObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight: detected and tracked objects from LiDAR and depth data."
]

// Actions
instance ActionACCTargetSpeed : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action: ACC sets target speed based on lead vehicle distance."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ActionEnableACC : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action: enable Adaptive Cruise Control."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ActionDisableACC : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action: disable Adaptive Cruise Control."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ActionSetTargetSpeed : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action: set desired target speed for ACC."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ActionEmergencyBrake : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action: trigger emergency braking command."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// Models
instance ACCBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "ACC behavior model: finite state machine driving target speed and lateral position."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadingEnabler
]

instance GeometricRepresentationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric model: reconstructs ego vehicle and obstacles from LiDAR and depth point clouds."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler
]

instance AnalyticalReportGenerationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical report generation model: aggregates telemetry and produces PDF diagnostic reports."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DTS mandates sub‑100 ms clock synchronization accuracy (≈100 ms) for all underlying devices to enable computation off‑loading and satisfy safety‑critical vehicular standards. All Functional Entities (FEs) must maintain a common notion of time; the required accuracy is driven by hardware limitations. No explicit synchronization frequency or temporal granularity is specified beyond this 100 ms accuracy bound, though the system operates on that base for timestamp alignment of sensor data and control commands."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The overall orchestration follows the ISO 23247 Reference Architecture. Functional Entities are realised as independent ROS2 packages; each package contains one or more ROS2 nodes implementing the required functionality. The Physical Twin Entity implements automotive‑specific and device‑control FEs; the Digital Twin Entity implements data‑management and control FEs; the User Entity implements UI‑centric FEs. Communication between entities is provided by the ROS2 middleware over a Wi‑Fi network, enabling point‑to‑point and publish‑subscribe exchanges of sensor data, control commands, and visualization streams. Resource allocation and deployment are explicitly decoupled by deploying each FE on its designated hardware component, thereby ensuring clear ownership of functionality while enabling coordinated operation of the full DTS."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin design mandates high‑fidelity realtime simulation, quantified by a maximum network latency of 100 ms (Requirement R7) and clock synchronization jitter less than 100 ms (Requirement R8). Validation is achieved through cross‑checking simulated object trajectories against ground truth from a lidar‑based map, with a maximum positional deviation of 0.1 m under nominal conditions. Uncertainty is captured by propagating measurement noise in the Kalman filter; confidence intervals (95%) are logged. An automated test harness injects synthetic faults into the sensor data stream to trigger error‑detection routines; discrepancies exceeding 0.2 m trigger an alarm and roll‑back to safe state. Quality assurance includes nightly regression tests on the data‑storage schema, CI pipelines for FE unit tests with 90 % code coverage, and a periodic audit of data integrity using checksums stored in the SQL database."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Data translation and service orchestration are performed within an internal ROS 2 network leveraging DDS for inter‑component communication. Inter‑entity communication—including messaging from the Digital Twin to the Physical Twin and user commands—is carried over a Wi‑Fi network using UDP. The document does not provide specifics about bandwidth, latency, reliability, redundancy, or security mechanisms, suggesting a single‑hop Wi‑Fi topology linking sensors, processing nodes, and the user interface."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The system aligns with ISO 23247 Part 2 (Digital twin framework for manufacturing – reference architecture). It implements an entity‑based reference architecture comprising Data Collection and Device Control, Digital Twin, User and Observable Manufacturing Element entities, each realized through Functional Entities. The architecture may be instantiated with protocols such as OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA) as a concrete implementation platform."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adopts a multi‑layered safety and security strategy grounded in ISO 26262‑1:2018 and ISO 15622:2018.  ISO 26262‑1 defines the functional‑safety lifecycle – hazard analysis, risk assessment, ASIL determination, safety concept, and sustained verification and validation – which the system applies to its critical control modules (e.g., ACC logic).  ISO 15622:2018 specifies performance requirements for Adaptive Cruise Control, mandating fail‑safe operation, redundancy of sensor inputs, and tight latency bounds for safety‑critical loops.  Vehicle‑to‑everything (V2X) services are incorporated via 3GPP TS 22.185, which prescribes a security architecture that includes authentication (challenge‑response or X‑509 certificate exchange), key management (dynamic key provisioning), and message‑level encryption (e.g., AES‑128/256).  Access control is enforced through role‑based permissions on data exchange between the virtual cockpit and the vehicle domain, ensuring that only authorized entities can instantiate or modify digital‑twin models.  The model‑driven architecture described by Dalibor et al. enforces a strict separation between the virtual cockpit and the vehicle control layer, creating a safe boundary that isolates simulation errors from physical actuators.  Combined, these measures provide a comprehensive safety‑critical and cybersecurity posture for the Digital Twin system."
]
